items.title: How a discarded coffee cup helped solve a 24-year murder mystery

children.content: Twenty-four years after the killing of a Canberra grandmother a discarded coffee cup on a east coast road trip led to the arrest and convictions of her killers.

items.title: Award-winning artist accused of copying second artwork

children.content: Australian artist Jane Allan, revealed to have previously copied another painter's work, is being accused of a second case of imitation.

image.altText: An artwork featuring a human-like outline.

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

image.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: 'We all know each other': Why this local Canberra shopping precinct is thriving

children.content: Local shops are a distinctive feature of Canberra, but while some are thriving, others have fallen into disrepair and offer no sense of community for nearby residents.

gallery.altText: Man wearing a white jumper inside a bakery.

items.title: 'Left an enormous hole': Man sentenced after crash killed Canberra father

children.content: A Canberra man has been spared jail after being found guilty of negligent driving causing the death of motorcyclist and father Craig O'Neill in a crash two years ago.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: Seeking equality, Afghan cricketers head to world cup they cannot play in

children.content: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.

items.title: 'All eyes are on the performance' as Canberra to Bali flights take off again

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

items.title: ASIO will use 'full' powers when so-called ISIS bride returns

items.title: France and Vanuatu clash over remote Pacific island where 'god resides'

children.content: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

items.title: As Ebola spreads, Australia faces rise in 'biblical' disease risks

children.content: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

items.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

children.content: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: The alternative burial method that could soon come to Australia

items.title: Small designers hitting roadblocks trying to get support against Shein copies

items.title: Former Liberal vice-president Teena McQueen dies after short illness

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

items.title: Family of 17yo killed in dog attack want mandatory owner licences

items.title: 'Heartbreaking': Sydney boarding house to be turned into luxury flats

items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

items.title: Neil the seal is back bigger than ever — and that's a problem

children.content: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land and a video shows him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?

items.title: Showers to chase away the chill of Queensland's winter

children.content: A warm change will arrive in Queensland this weekend, along with scattered rain and warnings of strong winds in the north.

image.altText: Brisbane skyline on a rainy day

items.title: The public can soon see this album hidden away for 126 years

children.content: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

image.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

items.title: Will Congress demanding US military halt action in Iran end the war?

children.content: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.
